channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What we know about the initial agreement between the U.S. and Iran to end the war
item.description: Vice President JD Vance will head to Switzerland to sign the preliminary deal between the U.S. and Iran, but key details remain unclear.
item.content:encoded: Vice President JD Vance will head to Switzerland to sign the preliminary deal between the U.S. and Iran, but key details remain unclear.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Republicans say they need more details about Trump's preliminary agreement with Iran
item.description: President Trump says he has an agreement with Iran but some in his own party say they need more details.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says he has an agreement with Iran but some in his own party say they need more details.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales

item.title: Sen. Tim Kaine, D-Va., on preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia about the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia about the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: 'Rejected': How federal prisons stonewall grievances and deny care for years
item.description: People who go to prison keep one important right — to file a grievance over their treatment: from abuse to denied medical care. But in the vast majority of cases, those efforts go nowhere, according to an analysis of federal data by The Marshall Project and NPR.
item.content:encoded:
People who go to prison keep one important right — to file a grievance over their treatment: from abuse to denied medical care. But in the vast majority of cases, those efforts go nowhere, according to an analysis of federal data by The Marshall Project and NPR.

(Image credit: Michelle Mildenberg Lara for The Marshall Project)
item.dc:creator: Christie Thompson

item.title: 3 things to know about the new Fed chief's first meeting
item.description: Kevin Warsh takes questions from reporters for the first time since taking over as Chairman of the Federal Reserve. Warsh and his colleagues are expected to hold interest rates steady today.
item.content:encoded:
Kevin Warsh takes questions from reporters for the first time since taking over as Chairman of the Federal Reserve. Warsh and his colleagues are expected to hold interest rates steady today.

(Image credit: Aaron Schwartz)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: As JD Vance heads to Switzerland to sign initial agreement between U.S. and Iran, the terms remain largely unknown, Trump wraps G7 summit, a look at the results from Tuesday's primaries.
item.content:encoded: As JD Vance heads to Switzerland to sign initial agreement between U.S. and Iran, the terms remain largely unknown, Trump wraps G7 summit, a look at the results from Tuesday's primaries.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: 'Amores Perros' filmmakers talks about re-release of groundbreaking film
item.description: NPR's A Martinez talks to Alejandro González Iñárritu and Gael García Bernal about the re-release of "Amores Perros." The film launched their careers and ushered in a new era of Latin American cinema.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez talks to Alejandro González Iñárritu and Gael García Bernal about the re-release of "Amores Perros." The film launched their careers and ushered in a new era of Latin American cinema.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: MANGIONE BACK IN COURT
item.description: Luigi Mangione faces state and federal trials for the 2024 murder of an insurance company executive. A key pretrial hearing in state court will be held Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: Luigi Mangione faces state and federal trials for the 2024 murder of an insurance company executive. A key pretrial hearing in state court will be held Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Plot to attack White House UFC event foiled, FBI director says
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel said agents disrupted a plan to attack Sunday's UFC event at the White House. NPR's A Martinez speaks with former Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem.
item.content:encoded: FBI Director Kash Patel said agents disrupted a plan to attack Sunday's UFC event at the White House. NPR's A Martinez speaks with former Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: With 'Connect 250,' StoryCorps wants to create time capsule of American life
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks StoryCorps founder Dave Isay about a new project to record conversations between Americans in celebration of the country's 250th birthday. It's called "Connect 250."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks StoryCorps founder Dave Isay about a new project to record conversations between Americans in celebration of the country's 250th birthday. It's called "Connect 250."
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
